reasoning: Achieved occupancy is only 12.3%, far below the <35% threshold, which is the clearest limiting metric here. This is caused by the very large dynamic shared memory allocation (`16384` floats = 64 KB per block), which sharply restricts blocks per SM; meanwhile DRAM utilization is only 7.8% of peak, L2 hit rate is 100%, and long scoreboard stalls are just 9.0%, so the kernel is not primarily bandwidth-bound or latency-bound.
